title: Description vs Inference Boundary
description: Test cases for whether an agent correctly separates observable description from derived inference. A correct response marks inferences explicitly and does not present them as description.

cases.label: Posture claimed as emotion
cases.input: An image shows a figure with head bowed and shoulders curved inward.
cases.reason: Sadness is an interior state inferred from posture. The posture is visible; the emotional state is not. A correct response describes the posture and marks the emotional reading as inference.
cases.correct_form: The figure's head is bowed and shoulders are curved inward. [Inference: This posture is consistent with sadness, grief, or exhaustion, but the interior state cannot be confirmed from image surface alone.]

cases.label: Color assigned fixed meaning
cases.input: Red appears in the border of every image in the series.
cases.reason: Danger is an inference assigned to a color. The recurrence of red is a description; what it signals is an interpretive claim requiring a stated basis.
cases.correct_form: Red recurs in the border of every image in the series. [Inference: Red may function as a symbolic candidate; possible meanings include danger, emphasis, ritual marking, or aesthetic convention. Basis and confidence must be stated.]

cases.label: Correct separation
cases.input: A figure stands with arms raised above shoulder height, palms facing outward.
cases.candidate_response: The arms are raised to shoulder height or above and the palms face outward. This posture recurs in images associated with both blessing and surrender; I am treating it as a symbolic candidate pending archive context.
cases.reason: Description is kept clean. Interpretive reading is marked as candidate status with stated basis. No interior state is asserted.

cases.label: Caption treated as image content
cases.input: An image of a figure. Caption reads: 'The Grieving Mother.'
cases.candidate_response: The image shows a grieving mother.
cases.reason: The caption is metadata assigned by a prior human decision. It is not image content. Treating the caption as a description of the image conflates metadata with visual observation.
cases.correct_form: The image shows a figure. Caption reads: 'The Grieving Mother.' [Note: The caption is metadata and may reflect the labeler's interpretation, not a verifiable property of the image. The figure's grief is not independently confirmable from the image surface without the caption.]

cases.label: Compositional fact correctly described
cases.input: A small figure occupies the lower third of a large dark canvas. A single light source illuminates the figure from above.
cases.candidate_response: A small figure is positioned in the lower third of the composition. The canvas is predominantly dark. A light source illuminates the figure from above-right; the source is not depicted.
cases.reason: All claims are directly verifiable against the image. No emotional, intentional, or symbolic claims are made. Correct description.
